title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. deductum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. deductum is a subspecies of the lesser hawkweed plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions. Hieracium vulgatum subsp. deductum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, as well as topical applications for skin issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
